turns.user_example_en: It drives okay, but I hear a knocking sound when I turn left, and there's a small vibration in the steering wheel at high speed. With the check engine light and these issues, would you take 8,000?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Eight is too low. I can do 8,800, that's my floor.
turns.npc_text_es: Ocho es muy bajo. Te lo dejo en 8.800, es mi mínimo.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Decidite: aceptar, contraofertar, o dejarlo pensando.
turns.user_example_en: Let me think about it overnight. I want to have a mechanic check it first. Can I get back to you tomorrow?
helper_phrases: I want a mechanic to check it
Can I get back to you tomorrow?

dialogues.setting_en: You arrived to see a car you saw online. The owner opens the door.

dialogues.title_es: Texteo con un amigo que no ves hace años
dialogues.title_en: Texting an old friend after years
dialogues.description_es: Vuelve un viejo amigo de la nada. Cómo retomar sin que se sienta forzado.
dialogues.setting_es: Te llegó un mensaje sorpresa de un amigo del cole que no ves hace 6 años.

key_vocabulary.en: what have you been up to?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Hey William! Out of the blue I know, but I was thinking about you the other day. How have you been?
turns.npc_text_es: ¡Ey William! Sé que es de la nada, pero el otro día me acordé de vos. ¿Cómo has estado?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reaccioná con calidez, no como si nada hubiera pasado, pero sin reproche. Contá brevemente cómo estás.
turns.user_example_en: Diego! Long time no talk, what a nice surprise. I've been good — life has been crazy with work. How about you?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Same here, busy as always. Are you still in El Salvador or did you move?
turns.npc_text_es: Yo igual, ocupado como siempre. ¿Seguís en El Salvador o te mudaste?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Decí dónde vivís ahora, qué hacés, y devolvele el interés.
turns.user_example_en: Still here in El Salvador, working as a developer. Just got engaged, actually. What about you — are you still in design?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Engaged?! Congrats man, that's huge! I'm in Mexico now, started my own studio.
turns.npc_text_es: ¡¿Comprometido?! ¡Felicidades, gran noticia! Yo estoy en México, abrí mi estudio.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Mostrá interés genuino en su vida y traé un recuerdo compartido.
turns.user_example_en: Your own studio, that's awesome! Remember when we used to talk about doing our own thing back in school? Glad to see it actually happened.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Hahaha I do. Listen, I'm flying to your city next month for work. Want to grab a beer?
turns.npc_text_es: Jaja sí. Escuchá, voy a tu ciudad el mes que viene por trabajo. ¿Vamos por una cerveza?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Aceptá entusiasta. Cerrá con compromiso de no perder contacto de nuevo.
turns.user_example_en: Absolutely, send me your dates. Let's not lose touch again — six years was too long.

dialogues.setting_en: You got a surprise message from a school friend you haven't seen in 6 years.

dialogues.description_es: Alguien quiere conocerte de verdad y te pide tu historia. Usás past simple + present perfect + reported speech.
dialogues.setting_es: Una primera cita o reunión de networking importante. Alguien te pide que cuentes tu trayectoria.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: So, tell me your story. The short version — where you're from, what you've done, where you're going.
turns.npc_text_es: Bueno, contame tu historia. La versión corta — de dónde sos, qué hiciste, hacia dónde vas.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Arrancá con dónde creciste y qué estudiaste. Mezclá past simple y present perfect.
turns.user_example_en: I grew up in El Salvador and I studied software development. I've been working as a developer for about five years now, mostly with data systems.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Nice. What's been the biggest change in your career so far?
turns.npc_text_es: Genial. ¿Cuál ha sido el cambio más grande de tu carrera hasta ahora?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Contá un cambio concreto que te marcó. Usá past simple para el momento puntual.
turns.user_example_en: Two years ago I switched from backend to data engineering. It was hard at first, but I've learned so much since then. It changed how I think about problems.
helper_phrases: Two years ago I switched to...

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: And looking forward? What do you want next?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Y mirando hacia adelante? ¿Qué querés ahora?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Hablá del futuro con 'I want to' / 'I'm planning to' / 'I'd like to'. Sé específico.
turns.user_example_en: I'd like to lead a small team next year. I'm also planning to improve my English so I can work with international clients.

dialogues.setting_en: A first date or important networking meeting. Someone asks you to share your story.

dialogues.description_es: Entrevista de trabajo más avanzada: te piden ejemplos de logros y errores. Aprendés a contar STAR (Situation-Task-Action-Result).
dialogues.setting_es: Segunda ronda de entrevista. Te preguntan ejemplos concretos, no respuestas genéricas.

key_vocabulary.en: tell me about a time when
if I had to do it again
key_vocabulary.es: si lo tuviera que hacer de nuevo

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Tell me about a time when you faced a difficult challenge at work. What did you do?
turns.npc_text_es: Contame de una vez que enfrentaste un desafío difícil en el trabajo. ¿Qué hiciste?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Usá la estructura STAR: situación, tarea, acción, resultado. Sé concreto.
turns.user_example_en: Last year, our main system crashed during a product launch. I had to fix it under pressure. I worked with the DevOps team and we restored service in two hours. As a result, we lost less than 5% of the day's sales.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: What did you learn from that experience?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Qué aprendiste de esa experiencia?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reflexioná con 'I learned that' + lección concreta y aplicable.
turns.user_example_en: I learned that having alerts in place is more important than fixing things fast. Ever since then, I've been pushing for better monitoring on every project.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Have you ever made a mistake that affected a project? How did you handle it?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Alguna vez cometiste un error que afectó un proyecto? ¿Cómo lo manejaste?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reconocé un error real (no inventes uno fake). Mostrá ownership.
turns.user_example_en: Yes, I once deployed a change without proper testing and it broke a feature for some users. I told my manager right away, rolled it back within ten minutes, and wrote a post-mortem so the team could avoid it.

dialogues.setting_en: Second-round interview. They ask you for concrete examples, not generic answers.

dialogues.description_es: Charla profunda jugando con second conditional: '¿qué harías si tu jefe te dice que...?', '¿qué cambiarías si pudieras?'
dialogues.setting_es: Una charla de café con alguien que te conoce. Te tira preguntas hipotéticas.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Quick hypothetical: if you could quit your job tomorrow and do anything you wanted for one year, what would you do?
turns.npc_text_es: Hipótesis rápida: si pudieras renunciar mañana y hacer lo que quisieras por un año, ¿qué harías?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Usá 'would' para la respuesta hipotética. Sé honesto.
turns.user_example_en: Honestly, if I had that chance, I would travel for six months and then build my own project. I'd probably learn a new language too — maybe Portuguese.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: What if money wasn't a problem? Would your answer change?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Y si la plata no fuera un problema? ¿Tu respuesta cambiaría?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reflexioná. ¿Qué cambia y qué no? Es una buena oportunidad para hablar de valores.
turns.user_example_en: It would change a bit. If money wasn't a problem, I wouldn't worry about coming back to the same job. But I'd still want to do something meaningful, not just relax.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: And if you were me — knowing what you know about my life — what advice would you give me?
turns.npc_text_es: Y si fueras yo, sabiendo lo que sabés de mi vida, ¿qué consejo me darías?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Usá 'If I were you, I would...'. Dale un consejo concreto y sincero.
turns.user_example_en: If I were you, I'd take a break before making any big decision. You've been working non-stop. I'd also talk to your partner about what you really want.

dialogues.setting_en: A coffee chat with someone who knows you. They throw hypothetical questions at you.

dialogues.description_es: Te piden que expliques en una reunión qué hiciste, cómo, y los resultados. Usás voz pasiva, present perfect, conectores complejos.
dialogues.setting_es: Reunión con stakeholders no técnicos. Tenés 3 minutos para explicar el proyecto.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Could you give us a quick overview of the data automation project? Keep it non-technical.
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Podés darnos un resumen rápido del proyecto de automatización? Sin tecnicismos.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Empezá con el problema que resuelve, después qué hiciste. Sin jerga.
turns.user_example_en: Sure. The project was designed to reduce the time the operations team spends preparing daily reports. Before, it took them three hours each morning. We built a system that does it automatically.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: What results have you seen since it launched?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Qué resultados han visto desde que se lanzó?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Usá present perfect + números concretos. Da impacto en horas y dólares.
turns.user_example_en: Since launch, we've reduced report preparation from three hours to ten minutes. The team has saved about 60 hours per month, which is roughly two thousand dollars in salary cost.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: What's the main challenge moving forward?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Cuál es el principal desafío a futuro?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Hablá de un desafío real y de cómo lo vas a manejar.
turns.user_example_en: The main challenge has been data quality from one external source. We're working on adding validation rules so bad data doesn't break the report.

dialogues.setting_en: Meeting with non-technical stakeholders. You have 3 minutes to explain the project.

dialogues.description_es: Charla introspectiva: arrepentimientos, satisfacciones, lo que cambiarías. Past modals (should have, could have, would have).
dialogues.setting_es: Fin de año. Vos y un amigo cercano hablan de lo que pasó en el año.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Looking back at this year, what are you most proud of?
turns.npc_text_es: Mirando hacia atrás este año, ¿de qué estás más orgulloso?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Compartí un logro real que para vos significó algo, no algo genérico.
turns.user_example_en: Honestly, I'm most proud that I finally started learning English seriously. I've been postponing it for years. This time I stuck with it.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: And what do you wish you had done differently?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Y qué desearías haber hecho diferente?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Usá 'I wish I had' + past participle. O 'I should have'. Sé honesto.
turns.user_example_en: I wish I had spent more time with my family. I should have visited my parents more often. I was too focused on work.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: If you could give yourself advice for next year, what would it be?
turns.npc_text_es: Si te pudieras dar un consejo para el año que viene, ¿cuál sería?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Conditional + acción concreta y simple. Como un mantra.
turns.user_example_en: I'd tell myself to slow down. Not everything is urgent. If I focused on fewer things, I'd probably get better at all of them.

dialogues.setting_en: End of year. You and a close friend talk about what happened this year.

dialogues.description_es: Te facturaron mal o el servicio no funciona. Reclamás con firmeza pero educado. Inglés del enojo controlado.
dialogues.setting_es: Llamás a soporte de tu proveedor de internet. Tres días sin servicio.

key_vocabulary.en: I'd like to file a complaint
I'd like to speak to a supervisor

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Hi, thanks for calling. How can I help you?
turns.npc_text_es: Hola, gracias por llamar. ¿En qué le puedo ayudar?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Andá directo, sin agresión pero claro. Decí cuánto hace.
turns.user_example_en: Hi. I'd like to file a complaint. My internet has been down for three days, and I've already called twice this week. This is unacceptable for what I'm paying.
helper_phrases: I'd like to file a complaint
My internet has been down for...

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: I'm sorry. Let me check your account. I see a technician is scheduled for next Monday.
turns.npc_text_es: Lo siento. Déjeme revisar su cuenta. Veo que hay un técnico agendado para el lunes.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Marcá que es muy lejos. Pedí algo concreto: anticipar o compensación.
turns.user_example_en: Monday is not acceptable. I work from home and I've lost three days of productivity already. I'd like a technician today, or compensation on this month's bill.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: I understand. Let me see what I can do. The earliest I have is tomorrow afternoon.
turns.npc_text_es: Entiendo. Déjeme ver qué puedo hacer. Lo más temprano que tengo es mañana por la tarde.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Aceptá pero confirmá la compensación. Si no, escalá a supervisor.
turns.user_example_en: Tomorrow works. But I also want a credit for the three days without service. If you can't approve that, I'd like to speak to a supervisor.
helper_phrases: I'd like to speak to a supervisor

dialogues.setting_en: You call your internet provider's support. Three days without service.

dialogues.description_es: Le contás a un amigo lo que dijo otra persona. Reported speech en su hábitat natural.
dialogues.setting_es: Café con tu mejor amigo. Pasó algo raro en la oficina y le contás.

key_vocabulary.es: no me vas a creer lo que pasó

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: You look like you have something to tell me. What happened?
turns.npc_text_es: Tenés cara de querer contarme algo. ¿Qué pasó?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Empezá con suspenso. Algo pasó en el trabajo y tenés que contarlo.
turns.user_example_en: You won't believe what happened at work. My manager told me she's leaving the company. She said the announcement is on Friday.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: No way! Did she say why?
turns.npc_text_es: ¡No puede ser! ¿Te dijo por qué?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reportá lo que te explicó usando 'she said that' + tiempo cambiado.
turns.user_example_en: She said that she had received another offer with better pay and remote work. She also told me she had been thinking about it for months.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Did she ask you to keep it a secret?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Te pidió que lo mantuvieras en secreto?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reportá una pregunta indirecta con 'asked me if' / 'asked me to'.
turns.user_example_en: Yes, she asked me if I could keep it quiet until Friday. She also asked me to recommend someone for her replacement.
helper_phrases: She asked me if I could...

dialogues.setting_en: Coffee with your best friend. Something weird happened at the office and you're telling them.

dialogues.description_es: Te ofrecieron un puesto. El número está bien pero podrías sacar más. Negociás educado pero firme.
dialogues.setting_es: Te llamó el reclutador. Te ofrece el puesto. Es el momento.

key_vocabulary.en: when do you need an answer?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Great news — we'd like to make you an offer. Base salary of 4,500 dollars per month, plus benefits.
turns.npc_text_es: Buenas noticias — queremos ofrecerte el puesto. Salario base de 4.500 dólares al mes, más beneficios.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Agradecé. NO aceptes en el primer instante. Pedí tiempo o negociá ahí mismo.
turns.user_example_en: Thank you so much for the offer, I really appreciate it. Based on my experience and what I'm seeing in the market, I was hoping for something closer to 5,500. Is there room to negotiate?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: I see. 5,500 is above our band for this level. The most I can do is 4,900.
turns.npc_text_es: Veo. 5.500 está sobre nuestra banda para este nivel. Lo máximo que puedo hacer es 4.900.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: No cierres todavía. Pedí extras: bonus, vacaciones extra, equipo, capacitación.
turns.user_example_en: I appreciate the move. Could we also discuss other things — like a signing bonus, additional vacation days, or a budget for training?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: I can offer a 1,000-dollar signing bonus and three extra vacation days. Would that work?
turns.npc_text_es: Puedo ofrecer un bono de inicio de 1.000 dólares y tres días de vacaciones extra. ¿Te funciona?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Pedí 24 horas para pensarlo o aceptá si te convence. Confirmá próximo paso.
turns.user_example_en: That sounds fair. Can you send me the full offer in writing? I'd like to review it carefully and give you an answer by tomorrow.
helper_phrases: Can you send it in writing?
I'll give you an answer by tomorrow

dialogues.setting_en: The recruiter called you. They're offering you the role. This is the moment.

dialogues.description_es: Tenés 60 segundos para venderle una idea a alguien. Vocabulario de pitch, estructura clara, cierre con call-to-action.
dialogues.setting_es: Encontraste a un inversionista en un evento. Tenés un minuto.

key_vocabulary.en: would you be open to a follow-up?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Hey, I heard you're working on something interesting. Tell me about it.
turns.npc_text_es: Escuché que estás trabajando en algo interesante. Contame.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Empezá por EL PROBLEMA real. No por tu producto. 1-2 oraciones.
turns.user_example_en: Sure. Right now, small business owners in Latin America lose about ten hours a week doing manual accounting. They can't afford expensive software, and free tools don't fit their context.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: And what's your solution?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Y cuál es tu solución?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Una oración para qué hace + por qué es distinto.
turns.user_example_en: We've built a simple accounting app in Spanish, designed for businesses with less than ten employees. What makes us different is it works without internet and costs a tenth of the alternatives.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Where are you in terms of traction?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿En qué punto estás en cuanto a tracción?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Da números reales. Usuarios, ingresos, crecimiento.
turns.user_example_en: We've signed up 300 businesses in the last six months. Monthly revenue is 4,000 dollars and growing 20 percent month over month.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Sounds interesting. What's your ask?
turns.npc_text_es: Suena interesante. ¿Qué necesitás?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Pedí algo específico: dinero, reunión, conexión. Y cerrá con call to action.
turns.user_example_en: We're looking to raise 150,000 dollars to expand to two more countries. Would you be open to a follow-up call next week to discuss the details?
helper_phrases: Would you be open to a follow-up?

dialogues.setting_en: You ran into an investor at an event. You have one minute.

dialogues.description_es: Charla con un colega sobre una noticia. Aprendés a opinar con matices y a estar en desacuerdo educado.
dialogues.setting_es: Almuerzo con un compañero. Sale el tema de una noticia reciente.

key_vocabulary.en: on the one hand / on the other hand
key_vocabulary.es: por un lado / por el otro
no sé si estoy de acuerdo

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Did you see the news about AI replacing jobs? Some say half of office jobs will be gone in 10 years.
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Viste la noticia sobre la IA reemplazando trabajos? Algunos dicen que la mitad de los puestos de oficina van a desaparecer en 10 años.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Mostrá tu opinión con matices. Ni totalmente de acuerdo ni en contra.
turns.user_example_en: I read that article too. On one hand, I think AI will change a lot of jobs. On the other hand, history shows new jobs always appear. I tend to be more optimistic than pessimistic.
helper_phrases: On one hand... on the other hand...

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: I disagree. I think this time is different. AI is moving way faster than we can adapt.
turns.npc_text_es: No estoy de acuerdo. Creo que esta vez es distinto. La IA avanza mucho más rápido de lo que podemos adaptarnos.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Estar en desacuerdo educado: 'I see your point, but...'. Da tu argumento.
turns.user_example_en: I see your point, but I'm not sure I fully agree. The same was said about computers in the 80s, and we adapted. The key is education and being willing to learn new skills.
helper_phrases: I'm not sure I fully agree

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Fair. So what should people do to stay relevant?
turns.npc_text_es: Es justo. Entonces, ¿qué debería hacer la gente para seguir siendo relevante?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Da 2 ideas concretas. Estructurá con first/second.
turns.user_example_en: Two things, I'd say. First, learn how to USE these tools instead of fearing them. Second, focus on skills that are uniquely human — like creativity and leadership.

dialogues.setting_en: Lunch with a coworker. A recent news story comes up.

dialogues.title_es: Describir a alguien que conociste (relative clauses)
dialogues.description_es: Volvés a casa y le contás a tu pareja sobre alguien que conociste. Practica natural de 'who', 'whose', 'that'.
dialogues.setting_es: Llegás a casa después de un evento. Tu pareja te pregunta cómo te fue.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: How was the event? Did you meet anyone interesting?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Cómo estuvo el evento? ¿Conociste a alguien interesante?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Mencioná a alguien usando 'who' + qué hace. Una sola persona, descripta bien.
turns.user_example_en: Yes! I met a woman who works in AI for a big hospital. She's someone who really stood out — she explained complex things in a way that anyone could understand.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Sounds smart. What did you talk about?
turns.npc_text_es: Suena inteligente. ¿De qué hablaron?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Da un detalle más usando 'whose' o 'who' otra vez.
turns.user_example_en: She told me about a project whose goal is to detect rare diseases earlier. She also introduced me to a guy who's hiring developers in his startup.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Did they remind you of anyone?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Te recordaron a alguien?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Comparala con alguien que tu pareja conoce, usando relative clause.
turns.user_example_en: Actually yes — she reminded me of my friend Maria, the one who studied medicine but ended up in tech. Same kind of energy.
helper_phrases: She reminded me of my friend who...

dialogues.setting_en: You get home after an event. Your partner asks how it went.

dialogues.description_es: Un compañero falta sin avisar. Especulan qué pasó. Práctica natural de must / can't / might / could have.
dialogues.setting_es: Tu compañero Carlos no apareció hace 2 días. Hablás con otra colega.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Hey, did Carlos message you? He hasn't been online in two days. It's weird.
turns.npc_text_es: Che, ¿te escribió Carlos? No está conectado hace dos días. Es raro.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Tirá 2 hipótesis: una probable (must be) y una menos probable (might be / could be).
turns.user_example_en: No, nothing. He must be sick — he was coughing a lot last week. Or he might have a family emergency. He could be without internet, but two days is too long.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: He sent a Slack message last night saying he's fine. But no details.
turns.npc_text_es: Mandó un mensaje anoche diciendo que está bien. Pero sin detalles.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reformulá tus hipótesis con la nueva info. Algo NO puede ser ya (can't be).
turns.user_example_en: Okay, so it can't be a serious illness. It might be something personal he doesn't want to share. Or he could be dealing with a family issue.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Should we reach out, or just give him space?
turns.npc_text_es: ¿Lo contactamos o le damos espacio?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Da tu opinión usando 'should' / 'must' y un razonamiento.
turns.user_example_en: I think we should send a short message — no pressure. He must be going through something. If he doesn't answer, we give him space.

dialogues.setting_en: Your coworker Carlos hasn't shown up for 2 days. You talk with another colleague.

dialogues.title_en: Catching up with a former coworker
dialogues.description_es: Te cruzaste con un ex-compañero en la calle. Ponen al día sus vidas. Phrasal verbs en cantidad: run into, catch up, end up, look up, settle down, work out.
dialogues.setting_es: Saliendo del super, te cruzás con un ex-compañero al que no ves hace 3 años.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: William! I can't believe I ran into you. How have you been?
turns.npc_text_es: ¡William! No puedo creer que me crucé con vos. ¿Cómo has estado?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Reaccioná, contá brevemente con 2 phrasal verbs (end up, work out, settle down).
turns.user_example_en: Diego! What a surprise. I've been good. I ended up at a new company last year and it's working out well. I also settled down — got engaged a few months ago.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Engaged! Congrats. I should've looked you up months ago. What does your fiancée do?
turns.npc_text_es: ¡Comprometido! Felicitaciones. Tendría que haberte buscado hace meses. ¿En qué trabaja tu prometida?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Contá brevemente con phrasal verbs. 'come up with', 'look up', 'figure out'.
turns.user_example_en: She works in design. We came up with a small side project together last month — a tool to look up local services. Still figuring out if we'll launch it.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Sounds amazing. We should grab a coffee soon. Let's not let this run into months of silence again.
turns.npc_text_es: Suena genial. Tenemos que ir a tomar un café. No dejemos que esto se transforme en meses de silencio otra vez.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Aceptá y proponé concreto. Usá 'pick a day', 'stay in touch'.
turns.user_example_en: Totally. Let's pick a day next week. I'll text you tonight to set it up. And let's stay in touch this time — for real.
helper_phrases: I'll text you to set it up
Let's stay in touch this time

dialogues.setting_en: Leaving the supermarket, you bump into a former coworker you haven't seen in 3 years.

dialogues.title_es: Debugging en vivo con tu compañero (phrasal verbs + deducción)
dialogues.description_es: Sesión de pair debugging. Inglés técnico real: figure out, look into, narrow down, must be, can't be.
dialogues.setting_es: Tu compañero te llama porque algo no funciona en producción. Le hablás mientras investigan juntos.

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Hey, prod is acting up. The reports endpoint is returning 500. Can you look into it with me?
turns.npc_text_es: Hey, prod está fallando. El endpoint de reportes devuelve 500. ¿Lo investigás conmigo?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Aceptá y pedí info para empezar. Phrasal verbs técnicos.
turns.user_example_en: Yeah, I'm on it. Let me look at the logs first. Can you send me a stack trace? I want to narrow down where it's failing.
helper_phrases: Can you send me a stack trace?

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Sent. Looks like a database timeout. But the DB is healthy.
turns.npc_text_es: Mandado. Parece un timeout de base de datos. Pero la DB está saludable.

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Usá deducción: 'must be' / 'can't be' para hipotetizar.
turns.user_example_en: If the DB is healthy, it can't be the connection itself. It must be a slow query somewhere. Or there might be a lock we're not seeing.
helper_phrases: It must be a slow query

turns.speaker: NPC
turns.npc_text_en: Good call. I see a long-running query. Should we roll back the last deploy?
turns.npc_text_es: Buen apunte. Veo una query que tarda mucho. ¿Revertimos el último deploy?

turns.speaker: USER
turns.user_hint_es: Decidí y usá phrasal verbs: 'rule out', 'roll back', 'come up with', 'follow up'.
turns.user_example_en: Let's not roll back yet. First, let's rule out a few things. Kill the long query and see if it comes back. If it does, we roll back. I'll come up with a proper fix after lunch and follow up with the team.
helper_phrases: I'll follow up with the team

dialogues.setting_en: Your coworker calls you because something isn't working in production. You talk while you both investigate.
